data.body:
[Plan review · PASS 1 · T1 prior-art / novel-architecture (overlay)] — PASS, no findings.

Assessment: This is a documentation-only ticket ("This is documentation only — no behavior changes"). T1 fires only when the plan crosses a bright-line by INTRODUCING new architecture (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth design, novel pattern, perf target, migration). This plan introduces none of those:

- The subsystems documented (`rebar.signing` HMAC surface, `rebar.llm.runner`/`workflow` runtime, `rebar.llm.prompts` contract model, `rebar.llm.findings` seam) are PRE-EXISTING and were built under prior epics — the ticket repackages them as a reference, not as new design. Documenting an existing security/signing surface does not re-trip the auth bright-line for the documentation task.
- The one new build artifact — the automated introspection-based drift-check test (AC-7, `tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py`) — follows a well-trodden in-repo convention. `inspect.signature`-based and "CI drift gate" tests already exist throughout the suite (e.g. `tests/unit/test_prompt_front_matter.py`, `test_execution_mode.py`, `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py`). No novel pattern, hence no Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging concern.

No unverified capability assertions of the "library supports X" kind are load-bearing here.

Affirmations (T1): (a) no novel architecture introduced — nothing to justify against prior art; (b) the drift-check mechanism reuses an established codebase convention rather than reinventing one; (c) no unresolved capability assertions.
